name: Gauri Kesani
ai_summary: Gauri Kesani has experience as an after-school teacher for K-5 students specializing in science and PE, along with babysitting experience. She uses calm intervention and emotional understanding to resolve conflicts, focusing on addressing underlying issues and teaching appropriate behavior.

transcripts.q3: My name is Corey khazzani. My experience with child care is being a after school teacher which I specialized in science and physical education for about a year and I worked with kindergarten through fifth grade. I also have a bit of babysitting experience
transcripts.q4: I live in Atlanta Georgia and I'm willing to commute about 15 to 25 minutes for a shift.
transcripts.q5: I would preferably like working part-time shifts because I am a college student and my schedule is very full. But if there's some letter 8 hours long, I would also be down to take those shifts depending on the day.
transcripts.q6:
So, the first thing I would do is intervene as soon as I realized that there is physical assault going on. And I would separate the children and speak to them in a calm voice. So they don't, you know, get upset from what I'm saying to them. So, just because I'm in a calm voice and try to deescalate by understanding, what happened and recognizing
 The children's emotions and trying to get them to take deep breaths to calm down and tell the kid who hit the other one that hitting is not, okay? And I understand that you can be mad, but hitting people is not okay at all to do.
 And sold the underlying issue because I'm sure there was something that happened that caused the hit to occur. So I would try and figure out what that is and resolve that and then maybe ask them if they think what a good solution would be.
